# Bot Builder - AI Reference

Bot Builder is a visual conversational bot framework. Users design conversation flows using 6 node types, connect them with conditional routes, and deploy bots via webhooks (WhatsApp, Telegram, or any channel).

This document describes everything needed to generate valid Bot Builder flow JSON.

---

## Flow Structure

```json
{
  "name": "string (1-96 chars, unique per bot)",
  "trigger_keywords": ["keyword1", "keyword2"],
  "variables": {
    "var_name": { "type": "STRING", "default": null }
  },
  "defaults": {
    "retry_logic": {
      "max_attempts": 3,
      "fail_route": "node_id_or_omit",
      "counter_text": "(Attempt {{current_attempt}} of {{max_attempts}})"
    }
  },
  "start_node_id": "first_node_id",
  "nodes": { "node_id": { "...node definition..." } }
}
```

### Top-level fields

**name:** Required. 1-96 chars, not whitespace-only, unique per bot.

**trigger_keywords:** Required. Array of 1+ strings. Each 1-96 chars. Allowed characters: letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, hyphens. Case-insensitive matching at runtime. Special wildcard `*` allowed but must be the sole keyword if used. No duplicates (case-insensitive).

**variables:** Optional. Object mapping variable names to definitions. Each definition has `type` (required) and `default` (optional).
- Types: `STRING`, `NUMBER`, `BOOLEAN`, `ARRAY`
- STRING default: must be string, max 256 chars
- NUMBER default: must be int or float (not boolean)
- BOOLEAN default: must be boolean
- ARRAY default: must be array, max 24 items
- Variable name rules: must match `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$` (start with letter or underscore, then alphanumeric/underscore)

**Reserved variable names (cannot use):** user, item, index, input, context, response, selection, true, false, null, success, error, _flow_variables, _flow_defaults, _api_result

**defaults:** Optional. Contains optional `retry_logic`:
- `max_attempts`: 1-10, default 3
- `fail_route`: Optional node ID to route to when max attempts exceeded. If omitted, session terminates.
- `counter_text`: Max 512 chars. Default: `"(Attempt {{current_attempt}} of {{max_attempts}})"`. Only place where `{{current_attempt}}` and `{{max_attempts}}` are valid template variables.

**start_node_id:** Required. 1-96 chars. Must reference an existing node ID.

**nodes:** Required. Object mapping node IDs to node definitions. 1-48 nodes. Node IDs must match `^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$` (alphanumeric and underscores only). Each node's `id` field must match its dictionary key.

---

## Node Structure

Every node follows this shape:

```json
{
  "id": "unique_node_id",
  "name": "Human-Readable Name",
  "type": "TEXT",
  "config": { "type": "TEXT", "...type-specific fields..." },
  "routes": [{ "condition": "expression", "target_node": "node_id" }],
  "position": { "x": 0, "y": 0 }
}
```

- **id:** 1-96 chars, `^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$`, must match dictionary key
- **name:** 1-50 chars, not whitespace-only, must be unique within flow (case-insensitive)
- **type:** One of: `TEXT`, `PROMPT`, `MENU`, `API_ACTION`, `LOGIC_EXPRESSION`, `SET_VARIABLE`
- **config:** Type-specific object. The `type` field inside config must match the node's `type`.
- **routes:** Optional. Array of route objects. If omitted or empty, node is terminal. Max varies by node type.
- **position:** Object with `x` and `y` numeric values (canvas coordinates).

### Route object

```json
{ "condition": "string (1-512 chars)", "target_node": "node_id (1-96 chars)" }
```

---

## Node Types

### 1. TEXT - Send a message

Displays text and auto-progresses to next node. Terminal if no routes.

```json
{
  "type": "TEXT",
  "config": {
    "type": "TEXT",
    "text": "Hello {{user_name}}!"
  },
  "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "next_node" }]
}
```

- **text:** Required. 1-1024 chars. Supports `{{variable}}` templates.
- **Routes:** 0-1 max. Condition must be `"true"`.

### 2. PROMPT - Ask for user input

Displays message, waits for user input, validates, saves to variable.

```json
{
  "type": "PROMPT",
  "config": {
    "type": "PROMPT",
    "text": "What is your name?",
    "save_to_variable": "user_name",
    "validation": {
      "type": "REGEX",
      "rule": "^[a-zA-Z ]{2,50}$",
      "error_message": "Please enter a valid name"
    },
    "interrupts": [
      { "input": "CANCEL", "target_node": "cancel_node" }
    ]
  },
  "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "next_node" }]
}
```

- **text:** Required. 1-1024 chars. Supports templates.
- **save_to_variable:** Required. 1-96 chars. Must be valid identifier, not reserved.
- **validation:** Optional.
  - `type`: `"REGEX"` or `"EXPRESSION"`
  - `rule`: Required. Max 512 chars.
    - REGEX: Valid Python regex. No lookahead/lookbehind (`(?=`, `(?!`, `(?<=`, `(?<!`), no named groups (`(?P<name>`). Must compile.
    - EXPRESSION: Supported: `input.isAlpha()`, `input.isNumeric()`, `input.isDigit()`, `input.length`. Operators: `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, `&&`, `||`. Context variables: `context.variable_name`.
  - `error_message`: Required. 1-512 chars.
- **interrupts:** Optional. Array of objects:
  - `input`: 1-96 chars, not whitespace-only. Case-insensitive matching at runtime.
  - `target_node`: 1-96 chars, must reference existing node.
- **Routes:** 0-1 max. Condition must be `"true"`.

### 3. MENU - Present options

Static (hardcoded) or dynamic (from variable) option lists.

**Static menu:**

```json
{
  "type": "MENU",
  "config": {
    "type": "MENU",
    "text": "Choose an option:",
    "source_type": "STATIC",
    "static_options": [
      { "label": "Option A" },
      { "label": "Option B" },
      { "label": "Option C" }
    ],
    "error_message": "Invalid selection, try again",
    "interrupts": []
  },
  "routes": [
    { "condition": "selection == 1", "target_node": "handle_a" },
    { "condition": "selection == 2", "target_node": "handle_b" },
    { "condition": "selection == 3", "target_node": "handle_c" }
  ]
}
```

- **text:** Required. 1-1024 chars. Supports templates.
- **source_type:** Required. `"STATIC"` or `"DYNAMIC"`.
- **static_options:** Required for STATIC. Array of 1-8 objects, each with `label` (1-96 chars).
- **output_mapping:** NOT allowed on STATIC menus (validation error if present).
- **error_message:** Optional. Max 512 chars. Shown on invalid selection.
- **interrupts:** Optional. Same as PROMPT interrupts.
- **Routes:** Max N routes where N = number of static options. Conditions must use the exact pattern `"selection == 1"`, `"selection == 2"`, etc. (this is a special pattern match, not a general expression). Best practice: include one route per option.

**Dynamic menu:**

```json
{
  "type": "MENU",
  "config": {
    "type": "MENU",
    "text": "Select a product:",
    "source_type": "DYNAMIC",
    "source_variable": "products_list",
    "item_template": "{{item.name}} - ${{item.price}}",
    "output_mapping": [
      { "source_path": "id", "target_variable": "selected_product_id" },
      { "source_path": "name", "target_variable": "selected_product_name" }
    ]
  },
  "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "next_node" }]
}
```

- **source_variable:** Required for DYNAMIC. Variable name containing an array. Max 96 chars.
- **item_template:** Required for DYNAMIC. Max 1024 chars. Supports `{{item}}`, `{{item.field}}`, and `{{index}}` (1-based). Also supports regular `{{variable}}` templates.
- **output_mapping:** Optional. Array of objects to extract data from selected item:
  - `source_path`: 1-256 chars. Dot notation only (no brackets). Path into the selected item object.
  - `target_variable`: 1-96 chars. Must be valid identifier, not reserved.
- **Routes:** 0-1 max. Condition must be `"true"`. For conditional branching after a dynamic menu, add a LOGIC_EXPRESSION node after it.
- Max 24 items rendered at runtime (array truncated if longer).

### 4. API_ACTION - Call external API

Makes HTTP request, extracts response data, routes on success/error.

```json
{
  "type": "API_ACTION",
  "config": {
    "type": "API_ACTION",
    "request": {
      "method": "POST",
      "url": "https://api.example.com/data/{{user_id}}",
      "headers": [
        { "name": "Authorization", "value": "Bearer {{api_token}}" },
        { "name": "Content-Type", "value": "application/json" }
      ],
      "body": "{\"key\": \"{{value}}\"}"
    },
    "response_map": [
      { "source_path": "data.id", "target_variable": "result_id" },
      { "source_path": "data.items.0.name", "target_variable": "first_item" }
    ],
    "success_check": {
      "status_codes": [200, 201]
    }
  },
  "routes": [
    { "condition": "success", "target_node": "on_success" },
    { "condition": "error", "target_node": "on_error" }
  ]
}
```

- **request:** Required.
  - `method`: Required. One of: `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`.
  - `url`: Required. 1-1024 chars. Supports templates. This is the only node type where `{{user.channel_id}}` and `{{user.channel}}` resolve (they render literally in other node types).
  - `headers`: Optional. Array of 0-10 objects. Each: `name` (1-128 chars), `value` (1-2048 chars, supports templates).
  - `body`: Optional. JSON string for POST/PUT/PATCH. Supports templates.
- **response_map:** Optional. Array of objects to extract data from response body:
  - `source_path`: 1-256 chars. Dot notation only. `*` = entire body, `*.0` = first array element, `data.items.0.id` = nested access.
  - `target_variable`: 1-96 chars. Must be valid identifier, not reserved.
- **success_check:** Optional. Determines if the API call succeeded:
  - `status_codes`: Optional. Array of HTTP status codes considered success.
  - `expression`: Optional. Max 512 chars. Condition expression to evaluate.
  - **If provided, at least one of `status_codes` or `expression` must be set.** An empty `{}` is invalid.
- **Routes:** 0-2 max. Valid conditions: `"success"` and `"error"`. Best practice: include both for robust flows. No routes = terminal node.
- **Constraints:** 30s fixed timeout. JSON responses only (non-JSON routes to error). 1MB max response body.

### 5. LOGIC_EXPRESSION - Conditional branching

Evaluates conditions to decide next node. No message displayed. Auto-progresses.

```json
{
  "type": "LOGIC_EXPRESSION",
  "config": {
    "type": "LOGIC_EXPRESSION"
  },
  "routes": [
    { "condition": "context.age >= 18 && context.verified == true", "target_node": "adult_verified" },
    { "condition": "context.age >= 18", "target_node": "adult_unverified" },
    { "condition": "true", "target_node": "minor" }
  ]
}
```

- **config:** Only the `type` field. No other fields.
- **Routes:** 0-8. Each condition must be a non-empty string. 0 routes = terminal node. Put specific conditions first, `"true"` catch-all last. See "Route Conditions Reference" below for expression syntax.

### 6. SET_VARIABLE - Assign values

Sets one or more variables. Auto-progresses.

```json
{
  "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
  "config": {
    "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
    "assignments": [
      { "variable": "full_name", "value": "{{first_name}} {{last_name}}" },
      { "variable": "status", "value": "active" }
    ]
  },
  "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "next_node" }]
}
```

- **assignments:** Required. Array of 1-8 objects:
  - `variable`: 1-96 chars. Must be valid identifier, not reserved. **Must be declared in the flow's `variables` section.**
  - `value`: 1-1024 chars. Supports templates.
  - No duplicate variable names within the same node.
- **Routes:** 0-1 max. Condition must be `"true"`.

---

## Route Conditions Reference

### Allowed conditions by node type

| Node Type | Max Routes | Allowed Conditions |
|-----------|-----------|-------------------|
| TEXT | 1 | `"true"` only |
| PROMPT | 1 | `"true"` only |
| SET_VARIABLE | 1 | `"true"` only |
| STATIC MENU | N (= option count) | `"selection == 1"`, `"selection == 2"`, ... (exact pattern) |
| DYNAMIC MENU | 1 | `"true"` only |
| API_ACTION | 2 | `"success"` and `"error"` |
| LOGIC_EXPRESSION | 8 | Any non-empty expression (0 routes = terminal) |

### Expression syntax (LOGIC_EXPRESSION conditions)

**Comparison operators:** `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`
- `==` and `!=` work with any types (strings, numbers, booleans, null)
- `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=` only work with numeric types (int/float). Booleans excluded. Returns false on type mismatch.

**Logical operators:** `&&` (AND, higher precedence), `||` (OR, lower precedence). Standard precedence: `a && b || c` evaluates as `(a && b) || c`.

**Variable access:**
- `context.var_name` — explicit context access with dot notation
- `var_name` — shorthand, resolves against context directly
- `context.nested.path` — nested access supported
- Missing variables return null (no error)

**Important:** The `context.` prefix works in route conditions but NOT in `{{template}}` variables. In templates, use `{{var_name}}` directly. `{{context.var_name}}` renders literally.

**Literals:**
- Strings: quoted with `"double"` or `'single'` quotes
- Numbers: `42`, `-10`, `3.14`
- Booleans: `true`, `True`, `TRUE`, `false`, `False`, `FALSE` (only these exact casings)
- Null: `null`, `None`, `none`, `NULL`

---

## Template Variables

Use `{{variable_name}}` in text fields. Dot notation for nested access: `{{user_data.name}}`.

| Variable | Available In | Description |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `{{var_name}}` | All node text fields | Flow variable value |
| `{{user.channel_id}}` | API_ACTION nodes only (url, headers, body) | User's platform ID |
| `{{user.channel}}` | API_ACTION nodes only (url, headers, body) | Channel name (whatsapp, etc.) |
| `{{item}}` | DYNAMIC MENU item_template only | Current item object |
| `{{item.field}}` | DYNAMIC MENU item_template only | Field of current item |
| `{{index}}` | DYNAMIC MENU item_template only | 1-based index of current item |
| `{{current_attempt}}` | retry_logic counter_text only | Current attempt number |
| `{{max_attempts}}` | retry_logic counter_text only | Max attempts configured |

**Not available as template variables:**
- `{{input}}` — NOT a template variable. `input` is only available in PROMPT validation expressions (e.g., `input.isAlpha()`, `input.length > 5`). Using `{{input}}` in any template raises an error.
- `{{current_attempt}}` and `{{max_attempts}}` — only work in `counter_text`, not in node text fields or error messages.
- `{{context.var_name}}` — does NOT work in templates. Renders literally. Use `{{var_name}}` directly.

**Missing variables** render literally (e.g., `{{undefined}}` stays as `{{undefined}}`).

**Unsupported in templates:** arithmetic (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`), default operator (`||`), ternary (`? :`), method calls (`.toUpperCase()`), bracket notation (`[0]`), control flow keywords.

---

## Constraints

| Limit | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Nodes per flow | 48 max |
| Routes per node | 8 max (varies by type, see table above) |
| Static menu options | 1-8 |
| Dynamic menu items | 24 max (runtime truncation) |
| Assignments per SET_VARIABLE | 1-8 |
| API headers per request | 10 max |
| Message text length | 1024 chars |
| Node name length | 50 chars |
| Node ID / variable name length | 96 chars |
| Flow name length | 96 chars |
| Route condition length | 512 chars |
| Validation rule length (REGEX or EXPRESSION) | 512 chars |
| Validation error message length | 512 chars |
| Menu error message length | 512 chars |
| Counter text length | 512 chars |
| API request URL length | 1024 chars |
| API header name length | 128 chars |
| API header value length | 2048 chars |
| API response path length | 256 chars |
| Menu output mapping path length | 256 chars |
| Variable default (STRING) length | 256 chars |
| Variable default (ARRAY) items | 24 max |
| Template/assignment value length | 1024 chars |
| Trigger keyword length | 96 chars |
| Interrupt keyword length | 96 chars |
| API timeout | 30 seconds (fixed) |
| API response body size | 1 MB |
| Session timeout | 30 minutes (absolute, not sliding) |
| Max auto-progression | 10 consecutive non-input nodes |
| Retry max attempts | 1-10 |

---

## Validation Rules

1. Required top-level fields: `name`, `trigger_keywords`, `start_node_id`, `nodes`.
2. `start_node_id` must reference an existing node in `nodes`.
3. All nodes except the start node must be targeted by at least one route (no orphan nodes).
4. Cycles are allowed only if they contain at least one input node (PROMPT or MENU). Cycles with only non-input nodes (TEXT, API_ACTION, LOGIC_EXPRESSION, SET_VARIABLE) are rejected.
5. Node names must be unique within the flow (case-insensitive).
6. Node IDs must match their dictionary keys.
7. The `type` field inside `config` must match the node's `type` field.
8. SET_VARIABLE assignments must reference variables declared in the flow's `variables` section.
9. Route target nodes must reference existing nodes.
10. `selection == N` conditions on STATIC MENU must have N in range 1 to number of options.
11. STATIC MENU cannot have `output_mapping`.
12. DYNAMIC MENU requires both `source_variable` and `item_template`.

---

## Complete Example: Pizza Order Bot

```json
{
  "name": "pizza_order",
  "trigger_keywords": ["PIZZA", "ORDER"],
  "variables": {
    "customer_name": { "type": "STRING" },
    "pizza_size": { "type": "STRING" },
    "order_id": { "type": "STRING" }
  },
  "defaults": {
    "retry_logic": {
      "max_attempts": 3,
      "counter_text": "(Attempt {{current_attempt}} of {{max_attempts}})"
    }
  },
  "start_node_id": "greet",
  "nodes": {
    "greet": {
      "id": "greet",
      "name": "Greeting",
      "type": "TEXT",
      "config": { "type": "TEXT", "text": "Welcome to Pizza Bot! Let's get your order started." },
      "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "ask_name" }],
      "position": { "x": 0, "y": 0 }
    },
    "ask_name": {
      "id": "ask_name",
      "name": "Ask Name",
      "type": "PROMPT",
      "config": {
        "type": "PROMPT",
        "text": "What's your name?",
        "save_to_variable": "customer_name",
        "validation": {
          "type": "REGEX",
          "rule": "^[a-zA-Z ]{2,50}$",
          "error_message": "Please enter a valid name (letters only)"
        }
      },
      "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "pick_size" }],
      "position": { "x": 0, "y": 100 }
    },
    "pick_size": {
      "id": "pick_size",
      "name": "Pick Size",
      "type": "MENU",
      "config": {
        "type": "MENU",
        "text": "Hi {{customer_name}}! What size pizza?",
        "source_type": "STATIC",
        "static_options": [
          { "label": "Small ($8)" },
          { "label": "Medium ($12)" },
          { "label": "Large ($16)" }
        ]
      },
      "routes": [
        { "condition": "selection == 1", "target_node": "set_small" },
        { "condition": "selection == 2", "target_node": "set_medium" },
        { "condition": "selection == 3", "target_node": "set_large" }
      ],
      "position": { "x": 0, "y": 200 }
    },
    "set_small": {
      "id": "set_small",
      "name": "Set Small",
      "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
      "config": {
        "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
        "assignments": [{ "variable": "pizza_size", "value": "small" }]
      },
      "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "place_order" }],
      "position": { "x": -100, "y": 300 }
    },
    "set_medium": {
      "id": "set_medium",
      "name": "Set Medium",
      "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
      "config": {
        "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
        "assignments": [{ "variable": "pizza_size", "value": "medium" }]
      },
      "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "place_order" }],
      "position": { "x": 0, "y": 300 }
    },
    "set_large": {
      "id": "set_large",
      "name": "Set Large",
      "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
      "config": {
        "type": "SET_VARIABLE",
        "assignments": [{ "variable": "pizza_size", "value": "large" }]
      },
      "routes": [{ "condition": "true", "target_node": "place_order" }],
      "position": { "x": 100, "y": 300 }
    },
    "place_order": {
      "id": "place_order",
      "name": "Place Order",
      "type": "API_ACTION",
      "config": {
        "type": "API_ACTION",
        "request": {
          "method": "POST",
          "url": "https://api.pizzashop.com/orders",
          "headers": [
            { "name": "Content-Type", "value": "application/json" }
          ],
          "body": "{\"name\": \"{{customer_name}}\", \"size\": \"{{pizza_size}}\"}"
        },
        "response_map": [
          { "source_path": "order_id", "target_variable": "order_id" }
        ],
        "success_check": { "status_codes": [200, 201] }
      },
      "routes": [
        { "condition": "success", "target_node": "confirm" },
        { "condition": "error", "target_node": "order_failed" }
      ],
      "position": { "x": 0, "y": 400 }
    },
    "confirm": {
      "id": "confirm",
      "name": "Confirmation",
      "type": "TEXT",
      "config": {
        "type": "TEXT",
        "text": "Order confirmed! {{customer_name}}, your {{pizza_size}} pizza is on the way. Order #{{order_id}}"
      },
      "routes": [],
      "position": { "x": 0, "y": 500 }
    },
    "order_failed": {
      "id": "order_failed",
      "name": "Order Failed",
      "type": "TEXT",
      "config": {
        "type": "TEXT",
        "text": "Sorry {{customer_name}}, something went wrong placing your order. Please try again later."
      },
      "routes": [],
      "position": { "x": 100, "y": 500 }
    }
  }
}
```

This flow: greets user -> asks name (with regex validation) -> offers size menu -> sets variable based on selection -> calls order API -> confirms or shows error. Triggered by sending "PIZZA" or "ORDER".
